scopeLimits: No app-visible real public records are added in v0.2.1.
Benchmarking and endpoint planning only; no scheduled automation is created.

scoreFactors: near-term relevance to v0.2.2 hospital pilot
risk of brittle scraping or unsupported assumptions

candidates.name: CMS Hospital General Information
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Does not provide live diversion, bed availability, transfer readiness, or service-line capacity.
Coordinates require geocoding or supplemental geometry.
candidates.rationale: Best first facility source: national official hospital listing with address, phone, hospital type, ownership and emergency-services indicators.

candidates.name: CMS Dialysis Facility - Listing by Facility
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Specialized facility type; not useful for emergency receiving capability.
Quality/service fields require careful consumer-facing definitions.
candidates.rationale: Official national dialysis facility inventory with addresses and phones; strong second-wave facility class after hospitals.

candidates.name: CMS Nursing Home General Information / Provider Information
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Not an acute-care routing source.
Quality and staffing measures have collection-period caveats.
candidates.rationale: Official active nursing home dataset; useful for long-term-care context and regional facility landscape.

candidates.name: CMS Provider of Services / Provider Characteristics
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Dataset naming and endpoint stability must be verified before relying on it.
May require filtering and interpretation across provider types.
candidates.rationale: Potential broad CMS provider cross-check if current downloadable fields are discoverable and stable.

candidates.name: CMS Long-Term Care Hospital General Information
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Not a live transfer-availability source.
Exact dataset endpoint should be pinned during ingestion.
candidates.rationale: Useful official facility class for post-acute/transfer landscape after hospital pilot is stable.

candidates.name: CMS Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility General Information
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Not suitable for patient-specific placement or transfer decisions.
candidates.rationale: Official IRF inventory candidate for rehabilitation destination awareness.

candidates.name: CMS Hospice General Information
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Not acute-care operational data.
candidates.rationale: Official hospice inventory candidate for regional planning context.

candidates.name: CMS Home Health Care Agencies
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: No live availability or acceptance data.
candidates.rationale: Official home health agency inventory candidate for longitudinal care landscape.

candidates.name: CMS Ambulatory Surgical Center Facility Data
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Limited relevance to emergency-care receiving capability.
candidates.rationale: Potential later facility class; lower priority for emergency/population-health prototype.

candidates.name: Census ACS 5-year demographics
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Requires careful geography/variable selection and margin-of-error handling.
candidates.rationale: Official demographic denominator/context source with API access and stable variable definitions.

candidates.name: Census Geocoder
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Batch limits and match quality require QA; do not silently accept low-confidence matches.
candidates.rationale: Official geocoding/census geography assignment service for converting CMS addresses to coordinates and geographies.

candidates.name: Census TIGER/Line geography files
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Large geometry files; requires topology/projection handling.
candidates.rationale: Authoritative geospatial boundary layer for future joins/maps, but heavier than first facility ingestion.

candidates.name: USDA RUCA tract and ZIP rurality files
candidates.agency: U.S. Department of Agriculture Economic Research Service
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Release cadence is not monthly; ZIP approximations can be less precise than tract assignment.
candidates.rationale: Useful rurality classification once census geography assignment exists.

candidates.name: CDC PLACES current release data
candidates.agency: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Modeled estimates; not individual diagnosis and not clinical decision support.
candidates.rationale: Official small-area public-health context for planning overlays after geography foundation.

candidates.name: CDC/ATSDR Social Vulnerability Index
candidates.agency: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention / Agency for Toxic Substances and Disease Registry
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Composite index requires careful explanation and non-stigmatizing display.
candidates.rationale: Official vulnerability index candidate for area-level context with explicit limitations.

candidates.name: SAMHSA FindTreatment / treatment locator source
candidates.agency: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Do not scrape if no supported bulk/API access exists.
Treatment availability and services can change rapidly and must be verified with providers.
candidates.rationale: High-value behavioral health candidate, but ingestion path must avoid unsupported scraping and respect locator terms/API availability.

candidates.name: NPPES/NPI monthly file
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Very large file; requires taxonomy filtering and careful entity-type handling.
Provider records are not facility capability records.
candidates.rationale: Authoritative FOIA-disclosable provider identifier file; useful for crosswalks, not as first app-visible facility source.

candidates.name: NPPES/NPI weekly incremental file
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Incremental logic is brittle until baseline normalization and idempotent merge tests exist.
candidates.rationale: Useful after monthly NPPES baseline exists to keep identifiers fresher.

candidates.name: State pharmacy board/license sources
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: No national standard endpoint; terms and fields vary widely by state.
candidates.rationale: Potential authoritative redundancy for pharmacies, but requires state-by-state legal and endpoint review.

candidates.name: State behavioral health licensing sources
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: No national standard endpoint; may require manual curation or per-state pipelines.
candidates.rationale: Potential official redundancy for behavioral health locations after federal options are evaluated.

candidates.name: State trauma designation lists
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: State definitions and designation levels vary; updates may be irregular.
candidates.rationale: Important future capability layer; must be sourced from state authorities and never used as live routing.

candidates.name: State stroke center designation lists
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Designation does not equal current availability or bypass status.
candidates.rationale: Important future capability layer when state official lists are available.

candidates.name: State STEMI/PCI designation lists
candidates.agency: State health departments / EMS or cardiovascular systems
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Terminology and validation rules vary by state.
candidates.rationale: Important future cardiac capability layer if official state lists can be maintained.

candidates.name: State perinatal designation lists
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: Not relevant to all regions; designation schemes differ.
candidates.rationale: Useful later for maternal/perinatal planning context where state official lists exist.

candidates.name: HIFLD or public geospatial facility layers
candidates.agency: Homeland Infrastructure Foundation-Level Data / public geospatial publishers
candidates.licenseOrUseNotes: Public official data; confirm terms during source-specific ingestion.
knownLimitations: May aggregate multiple upstream sources; authority and refresh date must be checked per layer.
candidates.rationale: Potential supplemental geometry/redundancy layer only; should not override source-of-truth facility agencies.

redundancyStrategy: Use CMS Hospital General Information as the primary first-ingestion facility source.
Use Census Geocoder only for address geocoding/geography assignment, with match-quality reporting and no silent low-confidence acceptance.
Do not use HIFLD or state lists as primary hospital authority in v0.2.2; reserve them for supplemental geometry or later capability cross-checks.
Do not ingest SAMHSA locator data unless a supported API/bulk access route is verified.
